associated_conditions: Hereditary breast and ovarian cancer syndrome
demo_variants.description: A well-known pathogenic frameshift variant in BRCA1 (185delAG), one of the most common founder mutations in Ashkenazi Jewish populations. Causes loss of BRCA1 protein function, significantly increasing breast and ovarian cancer risk.

The most common cause of cystic fibrosis worldwide. This in-frame deletion removes phenylalanine at position 508 of the CFTR protein, causing protein misfolding and degradation. Present in approximately 70% of CF alleles.

The APOE ε4 allele is the strongest common genetic risk factor for late-onset Alzheimer's disease. One copy increases risk ~3x, two copies ~12x. Also influences cardiovascular disease risk and cholesterol metabolism.

pharmacogenomics.clinical_impact: Individuals with two *4 alleles are poor metabolizers of CYP2D6 substrates. Codeine will not be converted to morphine (reduced pain relief). Tamoxifen may have reduced efficacy.
pharmacogenomics.prevalence: ~25% of Caucasians carry at least one *4 allele

demo_variants.description: The CYP2D6*4 allele is the most common non-functional CYP2D6 allele in European populations. It causes a splice defect resulting in no functional enzyme production. Critical for pharmacogenomic-guided prescribing.

One of the most common TP53 hotspot mutations found in human cancers. This gain-of-function mutation disrupts p53's DNA-binding ability, impairs tumor suppression, and is associated with Li-Fraumeni syndrome when inherited.
